_meta.description: Defense / aerospace electronics standards clause lookup. Hardware Lead + RF expert to review and expand. Severity = mandatory/recommended/informational.

clauses.short_title: Conducted Emissions, Audio Frequency Currents, Power Leads
clauses.description: Conducted emissions on power leads, 30 Hz to 10 kHz. Applies to submarines and Navy aircraft; ensures power-line ripple does not disturb low-frequency equipment.

clauses.short_title: Conducted Emissions, 10 kHz to 10 MHz, Power Leads
clauses.description: Conducted emissions on AC/DC power leads, 10 kHz to 10 MHz. Applies to all services; controls high-frequency ripple that would couple into other equipment via shared power.
DC and power-frequency susceptibility on power leads (30 Hz to 150 kHz).
Bulk-cable injection 10 kHz to 200 MHz. Simulates cable-coupled field energy into the UUT's harness.
Radiated magnetic-field emissions 30 Hz to 100 kHz. Submarine/Navy applicability.
Radiated electric-field emissions 10 kHz to 18 GHz. Critical for EW receivers and co-located emitters; governs housekeeping emissions of the UUT.
Radiated electric-field susceptibility 2 MHz to 40 GHz. Ground, ship, and aircraft platforms require 50 to 200 V/m depending on location.
Operating and storage high-temperature test procedures. Defense electronics commonly required to operate from -40 C to +71 C (ground) or wider (airborne).
Operating and storage low-temperature test procedures, typically -40 C or -55 C.
Vibration exposure for transport/operational environments. Profile depends on platform (e.g. fixed-wing jet, helicopter, tracked vehicle).
Functional and crash-safety shock testing (e.g. 40 g terminal-peak sawtooth, 11 ms).
Steady-state and transient requirements on 28 VDC aircraft power buses — including surge, dropout, and ripple.

clauses.short_title: Design Assurance Guidance for Airborne Electronic Hardware
clauses.description: Life-cycle guidance for complex airborne electronic hardware (FPGAs, PLDs, ASICs) with five Design Assurance Levels. Invokes requirements capture, traceability, verification, and configuration management.
Civil airborne equivalent of MIL-STD-461 RE102. Required for any airborne commercial/civil application.

clauses.short_title: Standard Interfaces of UAV Control Systems for NATO UAV Interoperability
clauses.description: Defines messages and interfaces for UAV control systems; relevant to UAV-borne EW/radar/comms payload interoperability with ground control stations.

clauses.short_title: Interoperability Standard for C-Band, X-Band, Ku-Band, and Ka-Band Satellite Terminals
clauses.description: Interoperability requirements for military SATCOM terminals, including modulation, coding, and link parameters.
Automatic Link Establishment protocol for HF radios (1.5-30 MHz). Fundamental for tactical HF comms.
Conducted emissions at transmitter antenna ports, 10 kHz to 40 GHz. Controls out-of-band/harmonic energy from transmitters.
Two-tone IMD susceptibility test at receiver antenna ports. Critical for co-site interference resilience.

clauses.short_title: Conducted Susceptibility, Rejection of Undesired Signals
clauses.description: Out-of-band signal rejection at receiver antenna ports. Verifies filter/preselector performance.
Cross-modulation susceptibility test for antenna-connected receivers.

clauses.short_title: Conducted Susceptibility, Bulk Cable Injection Impulse Excitation
clauses.description: Impulse excitation bulk-cable injection test. Simulates ESD/transient energy coupled onto the cabling.
Damped sinusoidal transient injection 10 kHz to 100 MHz on cables and power leads.
Pin-injection and cable-bundle tests simulating indirect lightning effects.

clauses.short_title: Radiated Emissions, Antenna Spurious and Harmonic Outputs
clauses.description: Spurious and harmonic radiated emissions from antenna port of transmitters.
Radiated magnetic-field susceptibility 30 Hz to 100 kHz. Navy and some Army applications.
EMP-style transient field susceptibility test. Required for shielded UUTs with platform-level EMP requirements.
Low pressure / altitude operational and storage testing. Required for airborne and high-altitude platforms.
Humidity exposure — aggravated and cyclic procedures. Required for equipment deployed in humid climates.
Salt fog corrosion test for equipment used near marine environments.
Blowing dust and sand tests. Required for ground platforms in arid environments.
Operation of electronics in fuel-vapor / explosive atmospheres. Critical for aircraft and vehicle fuel-proximate electronics.
Icing and freezing rain tests for exposed electronics (antennas, external sensors).
Steady-state, transient, and fault requirements for 115/200 VAC three-phase aircraft power.
Civil airborne RF susceptibility testing, 10 kHz to 40 GHz. Complement to Section 21 emissions.
Pin-injection and cable-bundle tests for indirect lightning effects on civil airborne equipment.
Human-body-model ESD test for civil airborne equipment accessible to personnel.
Mandates cryptographically secured IFF Mode 5 signals for NATO interoperability. Relevant to radar front-ends that implement IFF interrogation / transponder functions.
Motion imagery metadata and encoding standard for tactical ISR systems.
DAMA waveform requirements for military UHF SATCOM (FNBDP).

clauses.short_title: System Design and Engineering Standards for Tactical Communications
clauses.description: Baseline system-engineering requirements for tactical multi-channel radio.

clauses.short_title: Operation Within the Bands 902-928 MHz, 2.400-2.4835 GHz, 5.725-5.850 GHz
clauses.description: ISM unlicensed operation rules for frequency-hopping and digitally modulated systems.

clauses.short_title: Operation Within the Bands 902-928 MHz, 2400-2483.5 MHz, 5725-5875 MHz (Low Power)
clauses.description: Low-power unlicensed operation limits for ISM band devices.
Guide for selecting and using reliability prediction methodologies (MTBF/MTTR).
Cyclic humidity test for commercial/industrial electronics.

clauses.short_title: Characteristics of 28 VDC Ground Vehicle Power
clauses.description: Steady-state and transient requirements on 28 VDC vehicle power.
Class B / Class S screening sequence for microcircuits including burn-in, temperature cycling, and final electrical tests.
Military-grade microcircuit qualification level. Required for many programs of record.
Total Ionizing Dose test for space / rad-hard qualification.
